notes: Autonomous pre-flight checkout sequence executed. Rotor blade visual inspection via onboard cameras — passed. Firmware v2.1.3 confirmed via telemetry.

Scheduled 500-hour service. Robotic maintenance arm applied lubricant per procedure RT-204. Bearing play measured at 0.002mm via onboard sensor — within spec. Procedure commanded by Chen, Wei from JPL.
Flight control software updated to v2.2.0 via uplink during comm window. Includes improved vibration dampening algorithm. Post-upload self-test passed.
Dust accumulation reducing charge efficiency by ~8%. Autonomous compressed gas cleaning cycle activated. Post-cleaning efficiency restored to 97% per telemetry.

Scheduled 1000-cycle inspection via high-resolution onboard cameras. Image analysis by Rodriguez, Maria (ground team) identified microscopic edge erosion on Blade 2 leading edge — within acceptable limits per spec RT-105. Recommended monitoring at next inspection.
Battery health telemetry review. Capacity at 94% of original. Degradation curve tracking nominal per onboard diagnostics. No action required.
IMU drift correction uploaded and applied. Altimeter recalibrated via ground command sequence. Nav camera alignment verified using known landmark imaging.

Unscheduled inspection following Flight 38 vibration note. Bearing play measured at 0.003mm via onboard vibration-to-play inference model — approaching upper spec limit of 0.004mm. Robotic maintenance arm applied lubrication refresh. Ground team (Rodriguez, Maria) recommends planning bearing replacement at next opportunity.
Patch v2.2.1 uploaded via comm window to adjust vibration monitoring thresholds based on MA-008 findings. Alert threshold lowered from 0.20g to 0.186g. Post-upload self-test passed.

Follow-up imaging inspection commanded by Rodriguez, Maria. Blade 2 leading edge erosion has progressed slightly but remains within spec per image analysis. Blade 4 shows initial signs of similar erosion. Bearing play estimated at 0.0035mm from vibration signature analysis (Al-Rashid, Fatima). Recommend bearing replacement before Flight 55 — NOTE: upper rotor bearing assembly not available at Mars depot.
